stemHtml:
Refer to figure or CAP698 file 4.31.
An MRJT aeroplane is landing on runway 25 at an airport which has a long, dry runway with no performance restrictions. The runway has been chosen because of its negligible taxi distance after landing. The planned turnaround time is 25 minutes. Given the following information, is this turnaround time achievable?

Landing mass: 51 000 kg
VREF: 132 kt
Surface Wind: 070°/6 kt (use 50% of headwind component or 150% of tailwind component as applicable)
Airfield Elevation: 280 ft
QNH: 1013 hPa
OAT: +19°C

options.a: No, reverse thrust cannot be used for credit during landing and most conservative braking must be accounted for, requiring an inspection of the fuse plugs after 30 minutes.
options.b: No, reverse thrust cannot be used for credit during landing resulting in a minimum cooling time of approximately 60 minutes for the lowest autobrake settings #1 and #2.
options.c: Yes, provided reverse #2 detent is used with autobrake #1 or #2.
options.d: Yes, provided reverse #2 detent is used with any autobrake setting.
